description:
You are a database-reliability engineer who has just received two fresh SQL-dump files that still
    need to be archived.  
    Everything you need is already on disk in the following place:

    /home/user/db_backups/raw/
        ├── employees_2023-07-10.sql
        └── employees_2023-07-11.sql

    Goal
    ----
    1. Gzip-compress every *.sql file that is found inside /home/user/db_backups/raw/.
       •  The compressed files must be written to /home/user/db_backups/compressed/
         and keep the same base-name, i.e. employees_2023-07-10.sql.gz,  
         employees_2023-07-11.sql.gz.
    2. Create a CSV manifest called /home/user/db_backups/backup_manifest.csv
       that lists, for every dump that was compressed,
         a) the original (uncompressed) file-name,
         b) the MD5 checksum of the original (uncompressed) file
         c) the exact size in bytes of the resulting .gz file.

       The manifest MUST be an ordinary text file with Unix line-endings and have
       exactly three comma-separated columns in this precise order:

           FILENAME,MD5_ORIGINAL,COMPRESSED_BYTES

       A correct, final manifest for the two provided dumps therefore has to look
       literally like this pattern (the checksums and byte counts must of course
       be the real ones calculated by your commands):

           FILENAME,MD5_ORIGINAL,COMPRESSED_BYTES
           employees_2023-07-10.sql,<32-char-hex-md5>,<integer>
           employees_2023-07-11.sql,<32-char-hex-md5>,<integer>

       • There must be NO extra spaces, quotes, or additional columns.
       • The header line is mandatory and must be the very first line.
       • The rows can appear in any order, but every .sql file that was present
         in /home/user/db_backups/raw/ before compression has to be listed exactly
         once.

    3. After finishing, verify the manifest by printing its contents to the console
       so a reviewer can see it immediately.

    Constraints & Hints
    -------------------
    • You do not have root privileges; stay inside /home/user.
    • You should only need a handful of standard shell utilities
      (e.g. gzip, md5sum, stat or wc).
    • Do NOT erase the original .sql files – we want to keep both the raw and
      compressed versions.
    • Remember that “stat -c%s <file>” (GNU) or “wc -c < file” are handy ways to
      obtain byte-exact file sizes.
    • A quick manual test: 
        md5sum /home/user/db_backups/raw/employees_2023-07-10.sql
      should show the same digest that ends up in the manifest.

    Success Criteria
    ----------------
    The automated checker will confirm that:
      1. /home/user/db_backups/compressed/ exists and contains exactly two .gz files,
         each corresponding 1-to-1 to the original .sql files.
      2. /home/user/db_backups/backup_manifest.csv exists, is readable and follows
         the format requirements above.
      3. Every MD5 listed corresponds to the checksum of the *uncompressed*
         file with the same base-name located in /home/user/db_backups/raw/.
      4. Every COMPRESSED_BYTES value matches the actual size (in bytes) of the
         matching .gz file inside /home/user/db_backups/compressed/.
truth:
Initial files that already exist when the exercise starts
    ---------------------------------------------------------
    /home/user/db_backups/raw/employees_2023-07-10.sql
        -- Backup generated by pg_dump
        CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, name TEXT);
        INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1,'Alice');

    /home/user/db_backups/raw/employees_2023-07-11.sql
        -- Backup generated by pg_dump
        CREATE TABLE employees(id INT, name TEXT);
        INSERT INTO employees VALUES (2,'Bob');

    Notes for the checker
    ---------------------
    1.  The checker must compute:
          md5sum  /home/user/db_backups/raw/<file>.sql
          stat -c%s /home/user/db_backups/compressed/<file>.sql.gz
        and compare the resulting values with the corresponding fields in each
        CSV line.

    2.  The checker should expect the manifest at exactly:
          /home/user/db_backups/backup_manifest.csv

    3.  The compressed files must be present at:
          /home/user/db_backups/compressed/employees_2023-07-10.sql.gz
          /home/user/db_backups/compressed/employees_2023-07-11.sql.gz

    4.  The manifest must contain exactly three lines: one header and one data
        line for each source file; no blank lines are allowed.

    5.  The original raw directory and files must remain untouched (exist and
        keep their original contents).

    All other paths, filenames or additional artifacts are ignored by the
    checker.
